questions.question: Which phase is completed first in the Waterfall model?
options: System design and architecture
Requirements analysis and documentation
Unit testing and integration
Code implementation and review

questions.question: Agile methodology uses fixed-length iterations called sprints.
In a CI/CD pipeline, what typically triggers right after code is pushed?
options: An automated build and test sequence runs
The code is deployed directly to production
A manual review request is sent to the team
A standup meeting is scheduled for review

questions.question: In a GitHub Actions YAML file, the keyword to trigger a workflow on code push is: on: ___
How does Continuous Delivery differ from Continuous Deployment?
options: Delivery needs manual approval before production release
Delivery is fully automatic, Deployment needs manual approval
Delivery handles frontend, Deployment handles backend
There is no meaningful difference between the two

questions.question: Which AWS service is best suited for storing static assets like images and stylesheets?
options: RDS for structured asset storage
S3 for object-based file storage
EC2 for serving files from an instance
Lambda for on-demand asset delivery

questions.question: To check which branch you are currently on in git, you run: git ___
What is the purpose of the shebang line (#!/bin/bash) in a script?
options: It imports external bash library functions
It sets the file permissions to executable
It acts as a comment and gets ignored completely
It tells the system which interpreter to use

questions.question: In the cron expression "30 9 * * 1", at what minute does the job execute?
Which Jest function is specifically used to define a single test case?
options: describe() for grouping related assertions
expect() for matching output to expected value
test() or it() for defining one test case
assert() for validating a boolean condition

questions.question: Git hooks are scripts that run automatically before or after certain git events.
Which AWS CLI command creates a new key pair for EC2 SSH access?
options: aws ec2 create-key-pair --key-name mykey
ssh-keygen --provider aws --type ec2
aws iam generate-ssh-key --ec2-access
ec2-tools create-access-key --ssh-pair

questions.question: Where should API keys and credentials be stored in a GitHub Actions workflow?
options: Inside the workflow YAML as plain variables
In a .env file committed to the repository
In GitHub Secrets under repository settings
In a public config file at the repo root

questions.question: The Linux command to follow a log file in real-time and show the last 50 lines is: tail -f -n ___ /var/log/syslog
Which of the following is valid YAML syntax for a key-value pair?
options: key; value
key = value
key -> value
key: value

questions.question: A pre-push git hook can block a push operation if automated tests fail.
What does the command ssh-keygen -t ed25519 produce?
options: A TLS certificate for HTTPS connections
A GPG key for signing git commits
A symmetric encryption key for file transfers
An SSH key pair using the Ed25519 algorithm

questions.question: In a bash script, what value does the variable $? contain?
options: The path to the current home directory
The total number of arguments passed in
The process ID of the currently running shell
The exit status of the last executed command

questions.question: To install a package on Ubuntu using apt, you run: sudo apt ___
Which DevOps practice ensures every code change is automatically built and tested?
options: Continuous Integration for automated validation
Continuous Monitoring for production health
Infrastructure as Code for environment setup
Continuous Feedback for stakeholder alignment

questions.question: An S3 bucket name must be globally unique across all AWS accounts.
Which git command stages all modified and new files for the next commit?
options: git push --all to send all changes
git add . to stage everything in the directory
git commit -a to commit all tracked files
git stage --all to prepare the working tree

questions.question: The cron expression "0 */2 * * *" runs a job every how many hours?
What is the primary purpose of a Dockerfile?
options: To configure a web server like nginx
To define instructions for building a container image
To document the API endpoints of a service
To specify the test suite for a project

questions.question: In GitHub Actions, which keyword restricts a workflow to run only on the main branch?
options: target: main under jobs config
filter: main in the workflow header
only: main as a top-level directive
branches: [main] under the on trigger
